lesson: MIX LOW with full stock and syrup crusted around the float stem means the float is sticking high and lying about the level. Swap the switch — the inlet valve and controller are fine.
symptoms: MIX LOW alarm even with a full concentrate bag.
Bowl looks low but stock is definitely there.

tests.error-log: MIX LOW alarm re-arming within minutes of every restock.
tests.inspect-beater: Old crystallised syrup crusted through the wet side — around the spout tip, the drain seat and the float stem.

symptoms: Bowl runs itself nearly empty before anyone notices.
MIX LOW pops up only at the very end, then won't clear.
It keeps saying it's out of mix. It is not out of mix.
Staff have taken to slapping the side, which they say helps.

flavour: Technically, the machine was an optimist.
authenticityNote: Float switches on slushie mix reservoirs can stick in the raised position due to syrup residue, falsely reporting 'full' and cutting feed to the bowl.
